---
applyTo: "index.html,assets/**"
---

# Frontend instructions — Skovkrogen 37

## Styling

- Use **TailwindCSS utility classes** exclusively. The CDN build is loaded in `index.html`.
- Custom colours are defined in the inline `tailwind.config` block inside `<head>`:
  - Earth: `forest`, `peat`, `sand`
  - Water: `slate`, `teal`, `smoke`
  - Accent: `copper`, `rust`, `junebug`
- Never use raw hex values in class names — always reference the custom token names above.
- Font: `font-sans` resolves to Inter via the Tailwind config.
- Micro-labels: `text-[10px] uppercase tracking-[0.2em] text-rust font-bold`.
- Section headings: `text-4xl md:text-6xl font-bold uppercase tracking-tight text-sand`.
- Links and CTAs use `text-copper` / `hover:text-sand` with `transition-colors duration-300`.
- Borders are very subtle: `border-white/5` or `border-white/10`.

## Layout

- Full-viewport sections use the custom `scrolly-section` class (defined in `assets/css/styles.css`).
- The responsive grid uses `md:grid-cols-*` (the only breakpoint actively used is `md`).
- Max-width containers: `max-w-7xl mx-auto px-6`.

## JavaScript conventions

- Each script file begins with `'use strict';`.
- Feature scripts are wrapped in an IIFE: `(function () { … })();` (see `booking.js`).
- Init-on-load scripts use `document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', () => { … })` (see `gallery.js`).
- DOM element IDs follow the pattern `section-element` (e.g., `booking-form`, `gallery-grid`, `hero-image`).
- No frameworks — plain `document.getElementById`, `fetch`, `addEventListener`.
- Prefer `const` over `let`; never use `var`.
- Error messages and UI text must be in **Danish**.

## Booking form

- The form reads its API URL from `data-api-url` on `#booking-form`.
- Honeypot field (`name="website"`, hidden) — always include for bot prevention.
- Date inputs enforce `min` to today; checkout `min` syncs with checkin selection.
- Submit feedback: show/hide `#booking-success` and `#booking-error` divs; hide the form on success.

## Gallery

- `gallery.js` fetches `gallery_data.json` (generated by CI — never edit by hand).
- `cover` images set the hero background; `about` images set the about-section image.
- All other categories render in the `#gallery-grid` using a repeating column-span pattern.
- Use `encodeURI()` on image paths; set `loading="lazy"` and `decoding="async"` on `<img>`.
